query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) because it explicitly investigates "mass imputation" as a mechanism for integrating survey data and evaluates the resulting statistical properties (variance, efficiency) of the estimators, which directly pertains to the user's inquiry into how imputation methods impact statistical inference.
